text: **DrJill:** Hello, I'm Dr.
**DrJill:** Jill.
**DrJill:** Welcome to Let's Learn English with Anna.
**DrJill:** {Kids|子供たち} like to play {games|遊び/ゲーム}.
**DrJill:** But games are not just for {kids|子供たち}.
**DrJill:** Many people like to play games.
**DrJill:** And there are many {types|種類} of games, from {board games|ボードゲーム} and {computer games|コンピューターゲーム} to card games and {outdoor games|外で遊ぶゲーム}.
**DrJill:** What games do you play?
**DrJill:** Let's visit Anna.
**DrJill:** She helps us answer the question.
**DrJill:** What games do you play?
**Anna:** Today, I want to play a game.
**Max:** Let's play {computer chess|コンピューターチェス}.
**Anna:** No, Max, we played computer chess yesterday.
**Max:** You can play a {board game|ボードゲーム}.
**Anna:** I like board games, but my board games are all {old|古い}.
**Anna:** I want to play a new game.
**Anna:** Max?
**Anna:** Thanks.
**Anna:** You can play an {outdoor game|外で遊ぶゲーム}.
**Anna:** Daisy, it is hot today.
**Anna:** I want to play {indoors|屋内で}.
**Max:** Anna, you play games, but do not win.
**Max:** Why do you play?
**Anna:** Max, {winning|勝つこと} is not important.
**Anna:** We play to have fun.
**Anna:** You know, when I was a little girl, I was really good at {arcade games|アーケードゲーム}.
**Anna:** I know what game to play today.
**DrJill:** Anna never wins at computer chess.
**DrJill:** She always loses.
**DrJill:** Her {board games|ボードゲーム} are old.
**DrJill:** She wants to play a {new|新しい} game.
**DrJill:** And she wants to play {indoors|屋内で|室内で}, not outdoors.
**DrJill:** Always and never are {opposites|反対/正反対}.
**DrJill:** Win and lose.
**DrJill:** New and old are {opposites|反対/正反対} too.
**DrJill:** Now listen to some sentences.
**DrJill:** Listen for opposites.
**DrJill:** Her hands are clean.
**DrJill:** His hands are {dirty|汚れた}.
**DrJill:** She is big.
**DrJill:** He is {small|小さい}.
**DrJill:** My grandmother is old.
**DrJill:** My little sister is {young|若い}.
**DrJill:** Now it is your turn.
**DrJill:** Try to find the opposite.
**Students:** Now you find the opposite.
**Students:** What is the opposite of light?
**Students:** Dark.
**Students:** What is the opposite of thick?
**Students:** Thin
**Students:** What is the opposite of low?
**Students:** Hi!
**DrJill:** Now let's hear from some friends.
**DrJill:** They answer the {question|質問}: What games do you play?
**Students:** I like to play {hide and seek|かくれんぼ} with my friends.
**Students:** I love playing {cards|トランプ}.
**Students:** I always win.
**Students:** I never win.
**Students:** I love to play {chess|チェス}.
**Students:** I {taught|教えた/指導した|/tɔːt/|I taught my sister math.} my mom how to play {chess|チェス/西洋将棋|/tʃes/|Chess is a strategy game.}
**DrJill:** Thanks friends!
**DrJill:** Those all sound like great {games|ゲーム/遊び|/geɪmz/|We play fun games together.}!
**DrJill:** Now let's go back to Anna.
**DrJill:** She wants to play a game from her {childhood|幼少期|/ˈtʃaɪldhʊd/|I have memories from my childhood.}
**DrJill:** What game is she going to play?
**DrJill:** Let's find out.
**Anna:** They have {arcade|アーケード/ゲームセンター|/ˈɑːrkeɪd/|Arcade games are fun.} games!
**Anna:** Let's go!
**Anna:** When I was little, I played an arcade game called Maximum Overdrive
**Anna:** I won all the {times|回数/時|/taɪmz/|Many times I tried.}
**Anna:** I won!
**Anna:** I won the {high score|最高得点|/haɪ skɔːr/|He got the high score.}!
**Anna:** But my friends are not here.
**Anna:** Maybe {winning|勝つこと|/ˈwɪnɪŋ/|Winning is not everything.} is not important.
**Max:** Did you win?
**Anna:** Yeah, I won.
**Max:** Why are you {unhappy|不幸せ/不機嫌|/ʌnˈhæpi/|He looks unhappy today.}?
**Anna:** Playing alone was not fun.
**Anna:** So, do you want to play {computer|コンピューター|/kəmˈpjuːtər/|Computer games are popular.} chess?
**Max:** Yes.
**Max:** Yes, I do.
**DrJill:** Now you can ask your friends, what do you want to play?
**DrJill:** And remember, winning is not always important.
**DrJill:** Playing games is fun, even when you lose.
**DrJill:** See you next time on Let's Learn English with Anna!
